questions.text: You're planning a birthday party with a ₹2000 budget. Which approach shows responsible financial planning?

options.text: Allocate ₹1000 for venue, ₹500 for food, ₹300 for decorations, ₹200 for contingencies
Ask parents to cover extra costs

questions.text: You receive ₹1500 in gifts for your party. How should you handle the monetary gifts?
After the party, you realize you spent ₹500 more than planned. What's the best way to handle this?

options.text: Ignore it since it was special
Review what caused overspending and adjust future budgets

questions.text: A friend asks you to host another party. Your monthly entertainment budget is ₹750. What should you do?

options.text: Host a small gathering within your budget (₹750)

questions.text: You saved ₹1000 from your part-time job for a party. How should you evaluate if hosting is worth it?

options.text: Host the biggest party to impress
Compare the joy of hosting with alternative uses of ₹1000

result.winMessage: You got {{score}} out of {{total}} questions correct! You're learning smart party planning and financial decisions!
result.lesson: You understand the importance of budgeting, saving, and making thoughtful financial decisions for parties!
result.loseMessage: You got {{score}} out of {{total}} questions correct. Remember, smart party planning means budgeting and making thoughtful financial decisions!
result.tip: Try to choose the option that shows responsible budgeting and financial planning.
